claim: Enterprise AI adoption wall: accuracy is the #1 blocker for mission-critical workflows (compliance, underwriting, financial analysis); 95% of enterprise AI projects fail to reach production
actionable: When evaluating agent frameworks for enterprise, measure accuracy on multi-step tasks, not just single-step inference metrics
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: Compounding error effect: multi-step tasks amplify LLM mistakes; even if each step is 95% accurate, chaining 5 steps degrades compound accuracy significantly
actionable: Design multi-step agent workflows to minimize sequential dependencies; validate outputs at each step to catch errors before they compound
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: 'Prompt and pray' trap: high-automation LLM-controlled loops are easy to build but unreliable; manual static chains are rigid but give precision and control
actionable: For production enterprise workflows, favor static orchestrated chains with LLM calls at specific steps over free-form agentic loops
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: More constraints on agent tool use degrade success rate dramatically: 20-40 constraints cause sharp accuracy drop; even state-of-the-art models struggle with constraint compliance
actionable: Limit tool-use constraints to minimum necessary; break complex requirements into separate validation steps instead of jamming everything into one prompt
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: Maestro orchestration: creates structured deterministic plans (not prompts), ranks alternative courses of action by success probability, validates outputs, reports confidence per requirement
actionable: Use orchestration platforms that plan deterministically and provide per-requirement validation reports instead of free-form LLM reasoning
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: Distinguish logical plan (what steps) from operational plan (how to achieve them); operational plan depends on enterprise context, data, tools, resources, constraints
actionable: When designing agents, separate task definition from task execution; make execution context-aware by parameterizing available resources
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: Specialized judges (LLM as validator) for single constraints work better than monolithic validators; separate constraint checking improves reliability vs checking all together
actionable: Create task-specific validator models that check one requirement at a time instead of asking one validator to check all requirements together
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents

claim: Mythos (Claude frontier model in Maestro) achieved 77.8% on SweBench Pro vs Opus 4.6's 53.4%; represents step-function improvement in coding capability, not incremental
actionable: Track frontier model benchmarks on multi-step reasoning tasks (SweBench) as key indicator of agent reliability improvements
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Ori Goshen: Reliability Is the Bottleneck for Agents
